title: Quick & Dirty DNA Extraction
author: Sam Mantel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.baf7ibrn

steps.title: Pre-heat Quick & Dirty extraction buffer...
steps.instruction: Pre-heat Quick & Dirty extraction buffer with detergent in a 60 °C incubator.

steps.title: Place one steel ball bearing into each sample tube
steps.instruction: Place one steel ball bearing into each sample tube.

steps.title: When tissue is ground to a...
steps.instruction: When tissue is ground to a fine powder centrifuge briefly to remove powder from the inside of caps.

steps.title: Add roughly 200 mL nanopure H...
steps.instruction: Add roughly 200 mL nanopure H 2 O to a 1 L glass bottle with a stir bar and stir.

steps.title: Snap bottom back on to plate...
steps.instruction: Snap bottom back on to plate and shake in tissue homogenizer for 00:00:30 on highest seed. Note: Shake plate without lid so that casing of the homogenizer holds caps in place. Distribute samples evenly across plate so that samples are ground evenly.

steps.title: Add 350 µL pre-heated Q&D extraction...
steps.instruction: Add 350 µL pre-heated Q&D extraction buffer with detergent to each sample.

steps.title: Incubate samples at 60 °C for 00:15:00 to 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at 60 °C for 00:15:00 to 01:00:00 . Note: To prevent caps from popping off while incubating, place inverted plate bottom over caps and hold down with a weight.

steps.title: Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 22 °C, 20 minutes to pellet tissue
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 22 °C, 20 minutes to pellet tissue.

steps.title: Return to 1 L bottle and autoclave for 00:25:00
steps.instruction: Return to 1 L bottle and autoclave for 00:25:00 .

steps.title: Gently add 100 mL 5% sarkosyl solution to Q&D buffer
steps.instruction: Gently add 100 mL 5% sarkosyl solution to Q&D buffer. Note: Sarkosyl solution is a a detergent and will foam if poured to quickly.

steps.title: Near the end of the spin,...
steps.instruction: Near the end of the spin, transfer 200 µL per sample ice cold isopropanol to a new plate.

steps.title: Transfer 80 mL 1 M Tris base to bottle
steps.instruction: Transfer 80 mL 1 M Tris base to bottle.

steps.title: Transfer 56 mL 0
steps.instruction: Transfer 56 mL 0.5 M EDTA to bottle.

steps.title: Transfer 46
steps.instruction: Transfer 46.7 g NaCl to bottle.

steps.title: Transfer 3 g sodium metabisulfite to bottle
steps.instruction: Transfer 3 g sodium metabisulfite to bottle.

steps.title: Stir until dissolved
steps.instruction: Stir until dissolved.

steps.title: Transfer solution to graduated cylinder and...
steps.instruction: Transfer solution to graduated cylinder and add nanopure H 2 O to bring volume to 400 mL .

steps.title: Allow solution to cool to room temperature
steps.instruction: Allow solution to cool to room temperature.

steps.title: Swirl solution gently to mix. Note
steps.instruction: Swirl solution gently to mix. Note: Once detergent has been added, do not autoclave, detergent will degrade at high heat.

steps.title: Remove plate from -80 and quickly...
steps.instruction: Remove plate from -80 and quickly remove bottom from plate and float in liquid nitrogen to keep tissue frozen.

steps.title: Open tubes and discard caps
steps.instruction: Open tubes and discard caps.

steps.title: Cover with clean caps and invert gently to mix
steps.instruction: Cover with clean caps and invert gently to mix.

steps.title: Add 150 µL 1 M ammonium acetate to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 150 µL 1 M ammonium acetate to each sample.

steps.title: Invert samples gently every 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Invert samples gently every 00:05:00 . Note: Caps will likely be loose, close tubes completely before inverting.

steps.title: Place isopropanol, 70% ethanol, and 95%...
steps.instruction: Place isopropanol, 70% ethanol, and 95% ethanol in the freezer to chill.

steps.title: Transfer 300 µL , or as...
steps.instruction: Transfer 300 µL , or as much as you can, of supernatant from each sample to isopropanol plate.

steps.title: Recap tubes and invert several times to mix
steps.instruction: Recap tubes and invert several times to mix.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 to 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 to 00:15:00

steps.title: Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 4 °C, 20 minutes to pellet DNA
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 4 °C, 20 minutes to pellet DNA.

steps.title: Slowly pour off supernatant without disturbing pelleted DNA
steps.instruction: Slowly pour off supernatant without disturbing pelleted DNA.

steps.title: Add 500 µL ice cold 70% ethanol to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL ice cold 70% ethanol to each sample.

steps.title: Cover with clean caps and invert gently to mix
steps.instruction: Cover with clean caps and invert gently to mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes to wash pellet
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes to wash pellet.

steps.title: Slowly pour off supernatant without disturbing pelleted DNA
steps.instruction: Slowly pour off supernatant without disturbing pelleted DNA.

steps.title: Add 500 µL ice cold 95% ethanol to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL ice cold 95% ethanol to each sample.

steps.title: Cover with clean caps and invert gently to mix
steps.instruction: Cover with clean caps and invert gently to mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes to wash pellet
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes to wash pellet.

steps.title: Slowly pour off supernatant without disturbing pelleted DNA
steps.instruction: Slowly pour off supernatant without disturbing pelleted DNA.

steps.title: Place the flat on a paper...
steps.instruction: Place the flat on a paper towel and cover the open tubes with a KimWipe.

steps.title: Allow pellets to dry overnight
steps.instruction: Allow pellets to dry overnight.

steps.title: Once pellet is dry, and no...
steps.instruction: Once pellet is dry, and no residual liquid remains in the tubes, re-suspend pellets with 20 µL - 100 µL of TE buffer or nanopure H 2 O depending on pellet size, desired concentration, and future uses.
